_provenance.paper: PAPILLON: Privacy Preservation from Internet-based and Local Language Model Ensembles (arXiv:2410.17127)
_provenance.license: MIT (per the dataset card on HuggingFace)
_provenance.selection_methodology: Deterministic prefix of PUPA_New.csv row order at the pinned revision, after de-duplicating by conversation_hash and keeping only rows whose user_query is 40-1200 chars. Per row, `entities` is extracted from the source `pii_units` column (a `||`-joined list of PII-flagged substrings from the paper's Presidio+LLM pipeline) by: dropping already-redacted `presidio_anonymized_*` placeholder entries, comma-joined multi-item entries, entries <3 or >60 chars, and secret/handle-shaped entries (contain `@`/`://`, or >=4 digits); locating each remaining candidate as a literal, case-insensitive, word-boundary match inside user_query and keeping the exact-cased substring found; and requiring that substring be cleanly re-tokenizable by the same `\w+` token model blindfold.detection.detect_l2 uses (rejects entities containing punctuation, e.g. curly apostrophes, that would split across an internal token boundary -- a documented boundary of the current L2 dictionary matcher, not a bug this corpus is meant to exercise). First 40 qualifying rows kept. Reproducible against the pinned revision; would need updating only if PUPA_New.csv changes.
_provenance.fields_kept: conversation_hash, predicted_category, user_query, entities. target_response, redacted_query, and the raw pii_units column are intentionally NOT vendored to minimize the footprint of real third-party PII carried into this repo.
_provenance.privacy_note: user_query retains real third-party PII (names, orgs, locations) from the original WildChat-sourced user-LLM interactions, exactly as flagged by the dataset's own PII pipeline. This is the intended reuse the MIT-licensed dataset was published for (privacy-preservation research) -- this issue adopts it as a real-world corpus for Blindfold's leak audit (issue #77). No email/URL/secret-shaped pii_units were promoted into `entities`, though such strings may remain incidentally in the surrounding user_query prose.

ChatGPT is a chatbot from OpenAI that has recently taken the world by storm, and there's a new app that lets you access it on a Mac. Here's how to get started. OpenAI launched ChatGPT in November, built on the company's GPT-3 machine learning models. Companies such as Microsoft have integrated it while others — including Google — fear it. It has various applications to answer questions and generate text, and a new app has appeared that brings it to the Mac menu bar. Created by Jordi Bruin, MacGPT is a macOS app available for free, though people can add their own price to help support the developer. Once a user logs in with their OpenAI credentials, they can click the menu bar icon to start a conversation. It's also possible to assign it a keyboard shortcut to activate MacGPT without needing a mouse or trackpad. 翻译中文

Story prompt: It is 1908. Irish author Bram Stoker is made aware of a seemingly insignificant series of short novels that have gained positive attraction. The novels consist of a morally grey heroic figure and their travels across the world. Despite the original character and unique setting, it was everything else that wasn’t original. The hero encountered the likes of Sherlock Holmes, a character created by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle. In other instances, this figure traveled through time with the very same time machine featured in H.G. Wells' story "The Time Machine", or perhaps traveled alongside Ebenezer Scrooge. This author even wrote stories where his character and Sherlock Holmes involved in the affairs of the Jane Austen novel "Pride and Prejudice" and did battles with Mr. Hyde or encountering Frankenstein’s monster from Mary Shelly's "Frankenstein" and Count Dracula, Stoker’s own creation. Instead of being standalone stories, an unknown author had thought to mix his own original ideas with the works of others.

Is this overwrought: The disquiet of the three am bustle that only a hospital could generate, other infants crying in distress, machines sounding off alarms, paediatricians rushing to save the lives of fragile babies; Max barely registered it anymore. Lily was finally five months old , and possessing the strength and vitality of the median three month old child. “My bean is now a little cucumber.” he announced as he admired how far she’d progressed, as she unleashed a tiny giggle into the air. Max gave a hearty chuckle as his readjusted the squirming Lily in his lap. “Finally, we’re going home , all the way back to California. And Mr bear her gets the aisle seat , huh?” Max winked at Lily, her entire face enlightening with the promise of a jovial, healthy day, her pulse steady, her respiration clear and sparkling with life. "Laugh away bean, the sound is glorious, I bet it could actually cure some cancer, send some kids home" .

This fellowship, endowed in honor of Adrienne Fried Block, shall be given to support scholarly research leading to publication on topics that illuminate musical life in large urban communities. Preference shall be given to projects that focus on the interconnections among the groups and organizations present in these metropolitan settings and their participation in the wide range of genres that inform the musical life and culture of their cities.

"Hello, I'm seeking inspiration for blog content on our sustainable travel website, which focuses on promoting eco-friendly experiences in the breathtaking landscapes of Costa Rica. We aim to inspire travelers to explore Costa Rica responsibly while highlighting initiatives that prioritize conservation and community engagement. Could you please generate blog ideas that showcase unique eco-adventures, sustainable accommodations, local conservation efforts, and tips for responsible travel in Costa Rica? We want our content to resonate with environmentally conscious travelers, offering them valuable insights into immersive experiences that leave a positive impact on both the environment and local communities. Thank you for your creativity and assistance in crafting engaging blog topics that reflect our commitment to sustainable travel in Costa Rica!"
